page.content: The following table presents a comprehensive overview of various studies evaluating the biochemical parameters of patients subjected to different treatment modalities. Specifically, the focus is on the levels of aspartate aminotransferase (AST) measured in mU/mL, which serves as a critical indicator of liver function and cellular integrity. In particular, the data reveals notable differences in AST levels across different patient groups, including those categorized under Sham and BDL (Bile Duct Ligation) conditions. For instance, the average AST level recorded for the BDL group is significantly elevated at 647.7 ± 105 mU/mL, suggesting a marked hepatic response to the induced pathology.

subjected to different treatment modalities. Specifically, the focus is on the levels of aspartate aminotransferase (AST) measured
in mU/mL, which serves as a critical indicator of liver function and cellular integrity. In particular, the data reveals notable
differences in AST levels across different patient groups, including those categorized under Sham and BDL (Bile Duct Ligation)
conditions. For instance, the average AST level recorded for the BDL group is significantly elevated at 647.7 ± 105 mU/mL,
suggesting a marked hepatic response to the induced pathology.

page.content: Additionally, the table includes pertinent metrics such as the alloy composition used in the studies, with a specific reference to AA6060-T4. This alloy’s potential influence on patient outcomes is explored within the context of the studies, which encompass a total of several hundred patients, as indicated in the "No. of patients included" column. Collectively, these findings underscore the importance of both biochemical markers and material properties in understanding patient responses in clinical settings.
